frame_analyses.description: MSc Laparoscopic Surgery and Surgical Skills Programme promotional material shown. FLS peg transfer trainer visible with 6 pegs arranged in standard configuration. Two graspers visible on left side.
Close-up of peg transfer trainer showing rubber objects on left row of pegs. Pegs are clearly visible with proper spacing. Trainer mounted on white base.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard MISTELS peg transfer setup confirmed

frame_analyses.description: Institution director/faculty member presenting at Barts Cancer Institute, Queen Mary University of London. Slide background shows MSc programme information and learners performing FLS tasks.
Trainee in yellow shirt positioned at FLS trainer with hands visible. Left hand approaching pegs. Two monitor screens visible showing laparoscopic view.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Learner beginning task execution with proper positioning

frame_analyses.description: Trainee's left hand (non-dominant) using grasper to grasp first rubber object from left peg. Right hand holding second grasper positioned for assistance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial pickup phase - left hand controlling first object

frame_analyses.description: Object elevated above peg board by left grasper. Right grasper positioned to receive object. Good control maintained.
Right hand (dominant) placing transferred object onto right-side peg. Placement appears accurate with object seated properly.
Trainee continues with multiple object transfers. Rhythm appears steady. Objects being systematically transferred from left pegs to right pegs.
Faculty demonstration of proper technique shown on right monitor. Hands performing controlled object placement. Close-up view of proper grasp and placement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Expert demonstration of correct technique shown

frame_analyses.description: Equipment closeup showing Grasper brand laparoscopic instrument. Knot pusher tool visible. Standard MISTELS equipment verified.
Educational narration context with institutional signage shown (Charterhouse Square Campus, Barts Cancer Institute). Multiple frames of faculty lecturing about MSc programme.
Building signage and campus identification shown. Old Anatomy Building sign visible. Context confirms this is institutional educational material.

confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional is definitive based on: (1) Multiple frames showing faculty member presenting on stage with slides and institutional signage; (2) Promotional text about 'MSc Laparoscopic Surgery and Surgical Skills Programmes'; (3) Mix of brief training setup clips interspersed with lecture content and campus tour; (4) Educational narration context evident throughout. No single continuous performance attempt visible.
cannot_determine: Actual completion time - video is montage of multiple segments, not continuous performance
Individual phase timings - footage is edited and not sequential
Specific penalties - no unbroken performance to assess
video_purpose: Educational/promotional material for MSc programme at Barts Cancer Institute, Queen Mary University of London, highlighting FLS training curriculum and facilities

demonstrated_techniques: Peg transfer trainer setup and configuration
Dominant hand placement on contralateral pegs
Proper grasper handling and instrument control
verbal_cues: Video shows institutional context of MSc Laparoscopic Surgery programme
Integration of FLS MISTELS tasks in surgical education curriculum
common_errors_addressed: Maintaining instrument control throughout transfer sequence
teaching_content.instructional_focus: Programme overview and curriculum demonstration rather than detailed task instruction

recommendation: DO NOT SCORE - This is clearly instructional/promotional educational material, not a trainee performance. Frames show mixed segments of faculty presentation, institutional tours, equipment overview, and brief glimpses of training in progress. No single performance attempt is visible that could be reliably scored against FLS rubric.
